pattern: Backtracking over the digits 1 through 9 with a strictly increasing start index produces every size-k subset exactly once, pruning branches whose running sum already overshoots the target.
hints: The pool is fixed: only the digits 1 through 9, each usable at most once, so a combination is just a size-k subset of those nine digits.
Search with a start index so you only ever add a digit larger than the last one chosen, which yields each subset once and in increasing order.
Stop a branch early when the partial sum already exceeds n, or when even the largest remaining digits cannot reach n, then record a path once it has k digits summing to n.

edge_inputs.description: exactly one triple of distinct digits sums to seven
no four distinct digits can sum to one, so the answer is empty
the minimum possible sum for two digits
target below the smallest achievable sum for this k
all nine digits, only one combination reaches their total
target above the maximum achievable sum yields nothing

stress.description: every feasible k with a random reachable target so all branches are covered
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    k = rng.randint(2, 9)
    lo = k * (k + 1) // 2
    hi = sum(range(10 - k, 10))
    n = rng.randint(max(1, lo - 2), hi + 2)
    return [k, n]
stress.note: targets span just outside the achievable window so empty and non-empty results both occur
